NASA to Host Media Call with Artemis II Crew on Way Home from Moon

Media will have an opportunity at 9:45 p.m. EDT on Wednesday, April 8, to speak with NASA’s Artemis II crew members as they continue their journey toward Earth during their historic mission around the Moon. The 20-minute virtual news conference will take place with NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman,...

Real-time crew communication during lunar return provides unprecedented public engagement with deep-space mission operations and demonstrates NASA's commitment to transparency in human spaceflight.

It will become the carrier's new longest narrowbody service ever, but there's a twist...

Alaska Airlines' MAX transatlantic expansion signals carrier confidence in 737 MAX reliability while challenging traditional widebody economics on thin long-haul routes.

Pilots’ Group Warns of Conflict-Zone Strain

New position paper says repeated uneventful flights should not reduce scrutiny.

Pilot fatigue and complacency from routine conflict-zone operations may paradoxically increase risk despite accumulated safe flight hours.

FAA Budget Request Seeks 2,300 Controller Trainees

Agency ties proposed hiring increase to staffing, training and modernization needs.

FAA's 2,300 trainee request signals acknowledgment that controller staffing shortages require multi-year pipeline investment to address operational delays and safety concerns.

Part 5 compliance requires airlines to document and quantify safety risks systematically rather than rely on intuition, fundamentally changing how carriers approach hazard management.

Drones Apr 7, 2026

Major PX4 drone software vulnerability raises hijacking concerns

A widely used piece of drone software just got a serious cybersecurity wake-up call, and if you operate drones in the US, it’s something you’ll want to pay attention to. more…...

PX4 vulnerability exposes thousands of commercial and government drones to potential mid-flight control hijacking, creating urgent patching requirements across U.S. operators.

US Army launches Anduril Altius 700 drone from Apache for the first time

The US Army has for the first time launched a drone from an AH-64E Apache attack helicopter, firing…...

Integrating air-launched loitering munitions with existing Apache platforms expands force multiplier capabilities without requiring new aircraft procurement.

Airport Operator Sued Over Alleged Unpaid Revenue

City alleges missing fees, absent records and diverted tuition receipts at municipal GA airport.

Municipal airports face heightened financial scrutiny as cities demand accountability for revenue collection, potentially affecting operational budgets and service quality at GA facilities nationwide.

7 Reasons Why Premium Economy Keeps Expanding In 2026

Premium economy is a sweet spot for budget-conscious travelers who also want to have a comfortable journey.

Premium economy's expansion reflects airlines' shift toward higher-margin cabin density rather than just ultra-luxury, capturing price-sensitive business travelers and corporate travel budgets.

Military Apr 7, 2026

New Details Emerge About Rescue Mission Deep Inside Iran

New details were released during a White House press conference which described the rescue of the crew of DUDE 44, the F-15E Strike Eagle that went down in Iran. The rescue of the crew of the F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jet shot down over Iran has rapidly emerged as one of the most complex and […]...

Complex combat rescue operations in hostile territory require unprecedented coordination between military branches, establishing new operational standards for future downed-aircraft recovery missions.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain a leading fatal hazard despite pilot overconfidence in reaction time, necessitating enhanced detection systems and operational awareness protocols.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

A Look At The Salaries Of Pilots In Canada In 2026

Diving deeper into the economics of professional flying in the Great White North.

Canadian pilot compensation continues diverging from U.S. standards, creating potential talent retention challenges as regional carriers compete for experienced crews.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Starfish Space raises more than $100 million

Starfish Space has raised more than $100 million to scale up production of its satellite servicing spacecraft.

Growing investor confidence in orbital servicing infrastructure signals a shift toward space sustainability as a profitable, scalable business model beyond launch services.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

NASA’s new moon base project requires operational technology systems in space, but they are vulnerable.

Newly anointed NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman made a $20 billion announcement on March 24, 2026. To the delight of space enthusiasts everywhere, Isaacman said NASA was cancelling its project to deploy a space station in lunar orbit and would reorient to building a $20 billion base on the surface...

NASA's shift from orbital to surface-based lunar infrastructure prioritizes operational redundancy over traditional space station architecture, requiring aerospace suppliers to develop ruggedized systems with unprecedented reliability standards for sustained surface operations.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

First refurbished Jetstar 787 Dreamliner takes flight from Melbourne

Jetstar’s first refurbished Boeing 787 Dreamliner launched its inaugural service from Melbourne Airport (MEL) to Phuket International Airport…...

Jetstar's 787 refurbishment strategy enables premium long-haul capacity at lower costs, challenging full-service carriers on international routes.

General Aviation Apr 7, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Eastern Iowa Airport's mixed-use model supporting everything from flight training to commercial carriers demonstrates the economic value of regional hubs serving both general and commercial aviation markets simultaneously.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

British Airways Flew 13 Hours After Passenger Died Onboard - Foul Smell Complaints Followed

You might expect that, when a passenger passes away during a flight, pilots would divert the aircraft to an airport to deal with the situation. However, this isn’t always the case. Indeed, if a passenger dies of natural causes, airlines may just opt to have the flight continue onward to its...

Airlines operating under standard protocols may continue flights for 13+ hours after a passenger's natural death rather than diverting, creating operational and passenger experience challenges that warrant procedural review.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Up To 16-Hour Nonstop Flights: Iberia's 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Influenced by external factors, one very long link takes the crown, but what about the rest?

Iberia's ultra-long routes will test operational viability of 16-hour flights, requiring novel crew scheduling, maintenance planning, and passenger comfort strategies across their fleet.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

The Problem With Replacing Aircraft Like The Boeing 747

The biggest problem with replacing the Boeing 747 is simple: no single aircraft can do everything the 747 did.

The 747's unmatched combination of payload, range, and cargo versatility means airlines must now operate multiple aircraft types to replicate its operational flexibility, increasing complexity and costs.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Apr 7, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airline, airport, and flight systems offers airlines immediate efficiency gains without waiting for breakthrough propulsion technologies.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Earth observation operators push to deliver satellite images within minutes

Vantor employees were gathered for a sales kickoff in January, when an executive announced that a WorldView Legion satellite passing overhead would snap a photo of the California venue. Later, a buzzer sounded to alert the audience that the 30-centimeter-resolution image was available on the Vantor...

Real-time satellite imagery delivery is shifting from hours to minutes, enabling commercial applications that require immediate geospatial intelligence for time-sensitive decision-making.

See SpaceNews →

Military Apr 7, 2026

YFQ-42A Dark Merlin CCA Crashes in California During Test

GA-ASI confirmed a YFQ-42 crashed during take-off and testing has now been paused temporarily, with an investigation already launched. A prototype of the YFQ-42A Dark Merlin Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA), developed by General Atomics Aeronautical Systems, Inc (GA-ASI) crashed at the...

The YFQ-42A Dark Merlin crash during takeoff testing highlights risks in autonomous combat aircraft development and may impact the DoD's CCA program timeline and funding priorities.

See The Aviationist →

Wilson's departure after stabilizing Air India's operations signals potential leadership challenges or strategic shifts at the carrier during continued expansion.

Operational incidents in restricted airspace highlight the critical need for enhanced GPS and geofencing systems in rotorcraft operating near sensitive borders.

Australia’s JORN radar set to power Canada’s Arctic defense ambitions

Australia’s Jindalee Operational Radar Network is set to help Canada build the biggest and most advanced over-the-horizon radar…...

Australia's proven JORN technology transfer to Canada demonstrates how operational radar systems can be adapted for Arctic surveillance, setting a precedent for allied defense infrastructure modernization.

Manna expands drone delivery network with $50M boost

Ireland-based drone delivery company Manna Air Delivery is making a serious push into the US, and it just secured the funding to back it up. The company announced a fresh $50 million Series B round, bringing its total funding to $110 million, as it prepares to rapidly scale operations across the...

Manna's $50M Series B positions it as a well-capitalized competitor in the emerging US drone delivery market, signaling investor confidence in regulatory pathways for commercial autonomous delivery.

Drones Apr 7, 2026

DJI confirms end-of-support timeline for Mavic 2, Matrice 600 drones

Tech giant DJI will officially end service and support for several of its most beloved professional and prosumer drones in the coming weeks. That includes the iconic DJI Mavic 2 Pro, the versatile DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Zoom and DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Dual, and the heavy-lift powerhouse DJI Matrice...

DJI's support discontinuation forces operators of legacy professional drones to evaluate upgrade timelines and potential operational risks from unsupported systems.
